text: [warm, unhurried] Come in off the porch. Mind that first step — this house has settled a hundred and some years into the Savannah ground, and it remembers every foot that's crossed it. [gentle] My name is Wilder. This was my house. Still is, in the way that matters. [grave] Now listen close, I'll only say it the once. The city you're about to walk — Savannah — she does not forget. Every grief, every joy, every name the history books left out, she keeps them. Down in the brick. Down in the roots of these oaks. [softly] Most folks walk right over all that memory and never feel a thing. But your guide tonight — she knows how to ask the city to remember out loud. And when she asks, you'll hear them. The voices. [reassuring] They are not haunts come to trouble you. They are Savannah, remembering herself. [firm] So walk easy. You are a guest, not a target. Whatever you hear out there — it cannot follow you home. This house is a doorway out, and a doorway back. [blessing] Go on now. She's waiting for you in the park. [pause] And child — [softly] listen for the ones nobody else stayed to hear.
